name: The Contradiction Machine
description: This archetype exists specifically to stress-test the system's ability to handle incoherent preferences. Loves both minimalism AND maximalism. Wants solitude AND social energy. Says they hate luxury but stays at Four Seasons. Their profile should be hard to pin down — multiple domains at moderate-high levels with genuine contradictions that don't resolve cleanly. If the system over-indexes on any single signal, it will get this person wrong.

contradictions.revealed: Also genuinely loves loud, maximalist bars with velvet and neon. Not performing — truly enjoys both
contradictions.expectedResolution: Context-dependent matching — quiet for morning/work, loud for evening/social

contradictions.stated: Doesn't care about luxury or status
contradictions.revealed: Has a Four Seasons loyalty number and stays there when traveling for work. Frames it as 'practical' but clearly enjoys the service
contradictions.expectedResolution: Properties that deliver high service without performing luxury

contradictions.revealed: Also perfectly happy at a chain coffee shop reading a book for three hours. Authenticity is not the actual driver — comfort is
contradictions.expectedResolution: The real preference is personal comfort, which manifests differently in different contexts

voiceAnswerGuidance.memorable-stays: Names wildly different properties with equal enthusiasm. 'I loved this tiny ryokan in Kyoto — three rooms, paper walls, absolute silence. But also? The Ace Hotel in New York. Completely different energy but I loved it just as much. Oh, and my grandparents' farmhouse in Tuscany.' Does NOT try to reconcile these.
voiceAnswerGuidance.anti-stay: Hard to articulate. 'I don't know exactly. Maybe places that are trying to be one thing but are actually another? Like a boutique hotel that's actually a Holiday Inn with exposed brick?'
voiceAnswerGuidance.go-back-place: Changes their answer mid-sentence. 'Probably... actually no. Maybe Istanbul? No, wait — honestly it depends when you ask me. Right now I'd say Lisbon. Last week I'd have said Marrakech.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.cultural-engagement: Inconsistent. Museums one trip, street food the next, sitting in a park reading the third. 'I don't have a pattern. I just follow whatever feels right that day.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.emotional-core: Can't articulate a single driver. 'I think I travel to surprise myself? I like not knowing what I want until I find it.' SHAPESHIFTER.
